Unlike the 06-18 run (which was justifiably at the high), this refresh sits mid-range after a -23.4% drawdown.

live_fields.value: AS-OF business state at 2026-07-10, most-recent authoritative source still = Q1'26 6-K shareholder letter (2026-05-13); NBIS Q2 2026 6-K NOT yet out (expected ~late July / Aug 2026). CARRIED FORWARD unchanged: Microsoft up to $17.4B->$19.4B through Oct 2031; Meta ~$2.88B (Dec 2025, take-or-pay) PLUS up to ~$27B (Mar 2026: $12B firm Vera Rubin from early 2027 + $15B optional/resaleable) -- the $27B Meta contract is INTACT; RPO (audited) $33,585M at Q1'26; deferred revenue $4,778M; exit ARR $1.9B (Mar'26); YE2026 exit-ARR target $7-9B; FY2026 capex guided $20-25B; active (live) power last disclosed ~170MW YE2025 (Q1'26 & Q2'26 figures still WITHHELD -- the conversion-proof metric). NEW SINCE 06-18 (SENTIMENT/lead, NOT a filed change): (1) ~2026-07-01 a Bloomberg 'people familiar' report that Meta is standing up a cloud business to SELL EXCESS compute -> spotlights the pre-existing customer-concentration / K-B risk (a top-2 anchor could become a competing reseller); treated as an ELEVATED K-B watch, not a fundamental downgrade -- the Meta contract is unbroken. (2) Company build-out reported ON-TRACK/AHEAD: contracted power 3.5GW -> target >4GW; Missouri/PA/Finland sites on schedule -- no delay. PEER READ-THROUGH: Micron Q3 FY2026 (reported ~2026-06-24) showed HBM sold out through 2026 with demand > supply and HBM4 ramping for NVIDIA Vera Rubin -- i.e. the HIGH-barrier upstream (memory) stays tight, which does NOT relieve the LOW-barrier neocloud-RENTAL oversupply signal that NBIS sits in (capital-cycle lens).
